records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Ingrafted; p. pr. & vb. n. Ingrafting.]

defs: To insert, as a scion of one tree, shrub, or plant in another for propagation; as, to ingraft a peach scion on a plum tree;
to insert or introduce in such a way as to make a part of something.
quotes: This fellow would ingraft a foreign name Upon our stock.
A custom . . . ingrafted into the monarchy of Rome.

defs: To subject to the process of grafting; to furnish with grafts or scions; to graft; as, to ingraft a tree.
